# Payment Services Developer Portal

> REST API documentation and developer guides for the Payment Services platform — a payment processing system supporting cards, ACH, vaulting, subscriptions, refunds, and payment links.

## APIs

- [Payment Services API v1](/apis/openapi.yaml): Full OpenAPI 3.0 specification. Covers payments (sale, authorize, capture, void), refunds, subscriptions, vault/payment methods, customers, and payment links. Authentication via OAuth2 Client Credentials.

## Documentation

- [Getting Started](/docs/guides/): Authentication setup, credential requirements, sandbox vs production environments, and first API call walkthrough.
- [Tutorials](/docs/tutorials/): End-to-end implementation flows — card payments, vaulting, subscriptions, refunds, payment links.
- [Overview](/index.md): Portal navigation and API catalog.

## Key concepts for agents

### Authentication
All requests require two credentials:
1. Bearer token — obtained via OAuth2 Client Credentials at `/oauth2/token` using Basic Auth (base64 of `client_id:client_secret`). Expires after 3600 seconds.
2. `merchantToken` header — a GUID identifying the merchant on every request.

### Environments
- Sandbox: `https://sandbox.api.example.com/v1` (token: `https://sandbox.api.example.com/oauth2/token`)
- Production: `https://api.example.com/v1` (token: `https://api.example.com/oauth2/token`)

### Error handling
Standard HTTP status codes. Error bodies: `{ "errors": { "message": "...", "details": "..." } }`.

### Pagination
List endpoints use `page` (1-based) and `pageSize` parameters. No `totalPages` envelope — stop when result count < pageSize.

### Idempotency
Supply a `requestId` UUID header to enable idempotency and request tracing.

## Machine-readable specifications

OpenAPI 3.0 specs are available at:
- `/apis/openapi.yaml` — Payment Services API v1
